item.title: Take notes U.S., this is how you hold large corporations accountable.
item.description: The #EU has issued its first fine against #Apple over an investigation into allegations the tech-giant shut out #music-streaming rivals, including #Spotify on its platforms. The European Commission also ordered Apple to stop preventing music-streaming #apps from informing users of cheaper deals away from the #appstore
item.dc:creator: DarthPutinKGB

item.title: My heart is broken. May you rest in peace Jack...
item.description: My darling Jack passed away today. I rescued him as a kitten. He's been with me through the worst of my depression. He would crawl under the covers with me and cuddle. He was such a kind and gentle soul. And will be incredibly missed... May he rest in kitty heaven with all the treats his tummy can handle.
item.dc:creator: Scrumptiousassumptions

item.title: Executives learning the wrong lesson in 3,2,1..
item.description: https://www.pcgamer.com/gaming-industry/warner-bros-aims-to-increase-focus-on-free-to-play-and-live-service-games-because-disappointments-like-suicide-squad-make-the-big-budget-business-very-volatile/ Tldr, SSKTJL was a flop, so f2p cash grab games it is! The sad part is that WB mentions the success of hogwarts legacy and compares the big success and this game's failure to a high/low cycle and that's why they want f2p because it gives consistent revenue... I wonder if they ever considered that hogwarts legacy succeeded so immensely because it was a fun to play game without the micro transaction bullshit, whereas the suicide squad game is a boring, grindy looter shooter, with micro transactions.... If the game was good, it'd have made money. But yeah of course making f2p games is cheaper and easier and you can prey on people's gambling habits and / or idiots that spend thousands on digital bling bling dopamine hits /whether they can afford it or not)... So WB going that route means there won't likely be any decent LOTR, DC, or Nethetrealm games for the forseable future, but rather a slew of pachinko machines with a batman skins, woooooo!
item.dc:creator: Killerpixler

item.title: Burnt some CDs for one of my spouse's patients and got this letter in return
item.description: I don't tend to be my biggest fan and certainly haven't been this week, so this letter really made me misty eyed. I wrote him back to thank him for the thank you letter. Sounds silly but this was a wonderful gift, more than approximately 4 CDs if you ask me. FP edit- here is the track list for those interested: https://imgur.com/a/gfWSTz1
item.dc:creator: spsupjewski

item.title: It's not who I am underneath, but what I do that defines me.

item.dc:creator: TheReturnOfMadJuan

item.title: "Fight the good fight every moment, every minute, every day"
item.description:
You can't ask for power back, and unless you're a billionaire, you can't buy it back, the only way you can get it back is by *taking* it back.
I'm starting to think that this is my dad. That deep down he knows a lot of his conservative beliefs are built are piles of hateful bullshit, but he's got a new wife who's far-right, who has him going to a church that injects far too much right-leaning politics into its sermons, and he's the president of car club full of old conservatives, so for him to really dig deep into and possibly tear apart what he believes could destroy his entire social world, and sadly he fears that more than he fears being wrong.
I like my job but I have to work with people who sound like this
The flawless logic of the Christian right
item.dc:creator: CitrusyGarlic

item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.title: Things seen this week during structural assessments!
item.description:
Due to a landslide, this home is now severely undermined. The landslide spanned across three properties, causing extensive damage and posing safety risks to the affected area.
This slide also crashed into two homes situated at the bottom of the hillside.
Look, it floats.
The significant cracks and openings in this home suggest possible differential settlement.
Differential settlement is where one part of the foundation settles more than another due to soil conditions or other factors.
Hairline cracks are generally considered normal wear and tear. However, cracks measuring a quarter of an inch or larger, such as this one, can indicate structural issues. Basically, a finger should not be able to fit inside a crack.
Some debris that was left behind from a landslide. Implementing effective slope stabilization measures is recommended to minimize the impact of natural disasters and prevent potential damage.
These crooked joists have completely crushed the mudsill for this foundation. That’s just one thing wrong with this picture. Thank you for joining us, we hope you enjoyed! Check out our Instagram for more exciting content, and we'll see you next Tuesday at 2 pm! https://www.instagram.com/alphastructural/
item.dc:creator: AlphaStructural

item.title: First rule of smol kitty fight club, be incredibly cute
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Fresh dump from the bakery - Wednesday meme dump
item.description:
1. Funny, but I hope they didn't hurt eachother
2. Even if it's not a good job, you can still find ways to enjoy it
3. I seem to remember that they specifically told the actress she won't be involved in some stupid love plot or something and they re-wrote the script to be exactly this....
4. Useful boy
5. Funny dump, am I right haha
6. To all the non-Belles of the world, hang in there : )
7. I feel the Leslie Knope's of the governments deserve more appreciation. Without them I feel most countries will fall apart, while some idiot politician gets all the credit
8
9. Ewwww, human
10. The ending we deserved
11. We truly did
12
13. I like how he made this amazing thing, but with seat he was like "fuck it, I'l just grab the nearest chair"
14. What would be the spell to get jacked in the HP universe?
I wanted to share with you that as well as browsing and posting on Imgur all day I'm also making a passion project of mine. If you liked the humor in this dump I think you will like the game too called "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse). It is inspired by "The Stanley Parable" and similar in tone and features funny signs like this one here. Can you imagine? A first person game mixed with humor? https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ Anyway, It would mean the world to me and my brother if you checked the game out on Steam and gave it a wishlist. It won't disappoint. I promise. Just do not press the big red button, tho.
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Chocolate soufflé: Desserts around the world ep6
item.dc:creator: blondiebrowniesarethebestbrownies

item.title: He was only 33
item.description: He wasn't feeling well, fainted, and never woke up. It was exactly 17 months after my father died unexpectedly from a cardiac event. Almost a year after my aunt died. 6 months after I had to put down my cat. Is it possible to overload your brain on grief? WHY CAN'T I CRY FOR HIM?! Edit: This post was just meant to be my feeble yelp into the abyss. "Thankful" doesn't even begin to describe how I feel about this community. I'm almost overwhelmed at how kind, compassionate, honest, and uplifting Imgurians have been. And I'm thankful that so many people were willing to share their own grief. It's oddly comforting to know you're not alone when it feels like the world is crumbling around you. Somehow, you've managed to make another terrible day so much better. I really appreciate all the resources, articles, videos, and songs people shared. I've been diving in and trying to soak up all the information I can, and I've started a little playlist to try to help me through the coming months. I can't respond to all the comments, but I've read and upvoted all of them. Truly, I appreciate you all.
item.dc:creator: NeedingVsGetting
